info.title: authn.sh API
info.summary: Backend API (BAPI) and Frontend API (FAPI) for authn.sh.
info.description:
The authn.sh API has two surfaces:

- **BAPI** (Backend API) — server-to-server. Authenticated with a workspace
  secret key (`sk_test_…` / `sk_live_…`). Lives at `https://api.authn.sh/v1`.
- **FAPI** (Frontend API) — browser-facing. Identifies a device via the
  `__client` cookie set on the per-environment FAPI host
  (`https://{env_slug}.authn.sh/v1`). Cross-origin XHR is enabled for
  origins listed in the environment's allowlist.

This document is the source of truth for both surfaces. Every authn.sh SDK
(`@authn.sh/sdk-js`, `@authn.sh/sdk-react`, `authn/sdk` PHP, …) generates
its request and response types from the bundled artifact (`dist/openapi.bundled.json`).

The spec is split across `paths/` and `components/` for review-friendliness;
the bundler stitches everything together.

env_slug.description:
The slug of the environment whose FAPI you're addressing. Each
`Environment` row gets a unique slug at creation; production envs use
`<project_slug>`, development envs use `<project_slug>-dev`.

tags.name: Email Addresses
tags.description: User-owned email identifiers and their verification state.

tags.name: Environment
tags.description: Public per-environment configuration consumed at SDK boot (FAPI).

tags.name: Organizations
tags.description: Organizations within an environment. Populated in v0.2.

tags.name: Org Memberships
tags.description: User ↔ organization joins. Populated in v0.2.

tags.name: Org Invitations
tags.description: Pending invitations to join an organization. Populated in v0.2.

tags.name: Org Domains
tags.description: Verified domains attached to an organization. Populated in v0.2.

tags.name: Allowlist Identifiers
tags.description: Identifier patterns allowed to sign up under restricted mode.

tags.name: Redirect URLs
tags.description: URLs allowed as post-flow redirect targets.

tags.name: Webhooks
tags.description: Webhook endpoints, deliveries, and the event envelope.

tags.name: JWKS / OIDC
tags.description: Public keys and OIDC discovery served per-environment on the FAPI host.

bearer_secret_key.description:
Workspace-scoped secret key. Format: `sk_test_<…>` or `sk_live_<…>`.
Resolves to a single `Environment`. Issued and rotated from the
Dashboard or `POST /v1/api_keys` (post-v0.1).

client_cookie.name: __client
client_cookie.description:
FAPI device cookie. HttpOnly, `SameSite=Lax`, `Secure` in production.
Issued by `GET /v1/client` on first contact. Identifies the `Client`
row across requests on its FAPI host.

dev_browser_jwt.name: __authn_db_jwt
dev_browser_jwt.description:
Cross-origin development fallback for FAPI. When the SDK runs against a
dev FAPI host that can't set a SameSite=Lax cookie cross-site (no
HTTPS), it sends this query-string JWT instead. Production never uses
this.

Id.description:
A prefixed ULID. The prefix indicates the resource type
(`user_`, `org_`, `sess_`, `env_`, …); the suffix is a 26-character
Crockford-base32 ULID.

Metadata.description:
Free-form JSON object attached to a resource.

Three flavours exist:

- `public_metadata` — readable everywhere; writable only via BAPI.
- `private_metadata` — readable only via BAPI; never returned to FAPI;
  never logged or embedded in JWT claims unless an explicit
  `JwtTemplate` references it (post-v0.7).
- `unsafe_metadata` — readable everywhere; writable from FAPI by the
  end user themselves.

total_count.description: Total number of rows matching the filter, ignoring `limit`/`offset`.

PaginatedList.description:
Wrapper for paginated list endpoints. Concrete responses use `allOf` to
narrow `data[]` to the specific resource schema.

code.description:
Stable machine-readable code. SDKs match on this string; never
parse `message` or `long_message`.

long_message.description:
Long-form, end-user-friendly message including next-step guidance.
Safe to render in the UI.

meta.description: Per-code structured context (param name, attempt count, retry-after, …).

trace_id.description:
Server-generated request id. Same value goes into the audit log and
into the `X-Authn-Request-Id` response header, so customers can
paste it back to support.

Forbidden.description: Authenticated but not authorized for the action.

NotFound.description: Resource does not exist (or is not visible to the caller).

Conflict.description: State conflict — e.g. an `Idempotency-Key` reuse with a different request body.

Retry-After.description: Seconds until the next token in the bucket.

LimitParam.name: limit
LimitParam.description: Maximum rows to return. Default `10`, maximum `500`.

OffsetParam.name: offset
OffsetParam.description: Rows to skip before returning results.

OrderByParam.name: order_by
OrderByParam.description:
Sort key prefixed with `+` (ascending) or `-` (descending). Per-resource
endpoints document the allowed keys.

IdempotencyKeyHeader.name: Idempotency-Key
IdempotencyKeyHeader.description:
Caller-supplied UUID for `POST` mutations. The server caches the response
for 24 hours; replays with the same key return the cached response without
re-executing. A different request body under the same key returns `409`.
